key_insight: Pulsed plasma treatment densifies Fe–TiB2–CrB2 detonation coatings and improves hardness, wear, and corrosion resistance without changing the core phase set.
engineering_relevance: The process offers a practical post-treatment route to upgrade wear- and corrosion-critical coatings for demanding service environments.
method: Detonation-sprayed Fe–TiB2–CrB2 eutectic coatings were prepared.
Coatings were modified by pulsed plasma treatment (PPT).
Phase composition, microstructure, microhardness, wear, and corrosion behavior were assessed.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports retention of α-Fe, TiB2, and CrB2 phases, a 15–20% rise in near-surface microhardness to about 17–18 GPa, reduced wear depth, and a 0.15–0.20 V positive shift in corrosion potential.
watch_item: PPT improves surface performance, but process control is important because excessive energy input could alter the coating microstructure or induce unwanted phase changes.
paper_title: STUDIES OF THE STRUCTURAL AND PHASE STATE OF DETONATION COATINGS BASED ON THE FE–TIB₂–CRB₂ EUTECTIC SYSTEM AFTERPULSE PLASMA TREATMENT
